questionText: API latency is variable & app is sluggish. How do you design UI safely?

whatWorkedWell: Clearly articulated the concept of optimistic UI updates to keep the interface responsive during slow network calls.
Demonstrated strong knowledge of skeleton screens and shimmer loaders as loading state patterns.
Correctly identified debouncing and request cancellation as key techniques to avoid UI race conditions.
Gave a solid example of retry logic with exponential back-off to handle intermittent failures gracefully.
overallTakeaways: Consider elaborating on how you would handle error boundaries at the component level to prevent full-screen crashes.
Mention cache-first strategies (e.g. SWR / React Query) to immediately show stale data while refreshing in background.
Discuss the role of network quality detection (e.g. NetInfo) to adapt UI behaviour proactively.
Explore how you would test latency-related edge cases using tools like Charles Proxy or network throttling.

keyMoments.description: Correctly explained how public/private keys enable secure data transmission.
Clearly articulated that passwords can be easily compromised without encryption.
Accurately described the handshake process as fundamental to establishing security.
Struggled to name other security guarantees beyond confidentiality when prompted.
Recovered well by connecting TLS to real-world HTTPS usage in mobile apps.
